title: Production Audit Report
description: Output contract for a /production-audit run. Emitted by the skill, rendered by the site's ReportView. Bump meta.schemaVersion on breaking changes and update both sides in the same commit.

owner.description: Email that owns this report. The /reports collection shows a signed-in user only reports whose owner matches their session email; ADMIN_EMAILS see all. Public example reports may omit it.

auditedCommit.description: Git SHA of the audited working tree HEAD

dimensions.description: Dimensions this run assessed. Conditional dimensions the run resolved as inapplicable or unassessable belong in excludedDimensions, not here.

excludedDimensions.description: Conditional dimensions this run resolved out, each with why: not-applicable (the product has no such surface) or not-assessed (the surface exists but this run could not assess it, e.g. runtime-only checks on a static run).

runtimePass.description: Whether a live browser pass ran

skillVersion.description: Git SHA or tag of the skill repo at run time

version.description: The audited product's own release version (e.g. 0.1.0). When set, the report header stamps it instead of leading with the date, so a published report reads as the audit of a release rather than a stale dated run.

public.description: When true, this report is served ungated at /example/<slug> as a public credibility example and is listed on the public /reports index. Default false.

assessedScope.description: The evidence base the verdict rests on. Must be static when scope.runtimePass is false. A static verdict names its evidence base beneath the recommendation (static review, runtime not exercised) and lists the skipped runtime checks under notAssessed, so it never overclaims a runtime-verified result.

posture.description: One-line honest read of overall health, shown before the findings so a healthy app does not read as broken

justification.description: Opens by naming the assessed scope, then the reasons

blockingFindingIds.description: Findings that drove a non-safe verdict; empty for safe-to-ship. Every finding listed here must have verification.status confirmed or downgraded, never not-verified (enforced by make validate).

strengths.description: Verified-safe controls the audit confirmed are correct. Naming what is solid is part of an honest audit; rendered prominently before the findings. Not a place for problems.

note.description: What was verified and why it is sound

shipGates.description: Status of the pre-ship release gates (mobile, instrumentation) that cap the verdict. Report every gate the product's surface implies, even if only to say it was not assessed statically. A not-met gate caps the verdict below safe-to-ship.

note.description: What was checked and the basis for the status

findingIds.description: Findings that inform this gate's status

refuted.description: Dropped entirely; not present in findings[]

remediationPlan.description: Ordered by priority; index is the order

blastRadius.description: What could regress if this fix is wrong

deeperInvestigation.description: Issues needing architectural change or investigation beyond this audit

notAssessed.description: Checks this run could not perform (typically runtime-only checks in a static run)

howToVerify.description: The exact runtime check to run

findingId.description: Dimension prefix + zero-padded ordinal, e.g. SEC-01. Prefixes: SEC security, CON concurrency, REL reliability, A11Y accessibility, UI ui, INF infra, OPS operability, TEST testing-confidence, DATA data-migration-safety, SHIP release-safety, PERF performance-capacity.

dimension.description: Eleven dimensions with applicability resolved per project: the first six are usually applicable, the last five conditional, and a dimension with no surface here (accessibility on a headless service) is excluded with a reason, never silently skipped.

kind.description: risk: there is a path to harm in production (exposure, data or payment corruption, crash or unavailability, abuse, a hidden active failure). improvement: the code is safe today; this makes it more robust, observable, consistent, accessible, or faster, with no harm path. Only risks drive the verdict; improvements never cap it.

issue.description: What is wrong, one or two sentences

evidence.description: The code path or condition supporting the finding, citing file:line

confidence.description: Evidence class: runtime-reproduced (behavior observed live), code-traced (complete code path traced), configuration-confirmed (verified in config/infrastructure state), high-confidence (path traced with exactly one named unverified assumption), needs-verification (requires runtime/browser/multi-client observation; the exact check is stated)

assumption.description: Required when confidence is high-confidence: the one unverified assumption

status.description: Adversarial verification outcome; refuted findings are removed, not listed

method.description: How the skeptic was made independent of the finder. fresh-subagent is the default; high-impact findings should escalate to a more independent method

refutationNotes.description: What the skeptic checked; required for downgraded

instances.description: Additional occurrences of the same root cause

reproduction.description: Steps to trigger; only for runtime-reproduced findings with observable behavior
